# nxos_feature (Resource)

This resource can manage the feature configuration on NX-OS devices, including the administrative state of features such as BGP, OSPF, PIM, EVPN, LACP, vPC, and others, and feature-sets such as FEX, MPLS, and virtualization.

### API Documentation

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nxos_feature" "example" {
  analytics            = "enabled"
  bash_shell           = "enabled"
  bfd                  = "enabled"
  bgp                  = "enabled"
  dhcp                 = "enabled"
  evpn                 = "enabled"
  grpc                 = "enabled"
  hmm                  = "enabled"
  hsrp                 = "enabled"
  interface_vlan       = "enabled"
  isis                 = "enabled"
  lacp                 = "enabled"
  lldp                 = "enabled"
  macsec               = "enabled"
  netflow              = "enabled"
  ngmvpn               = "enabled"
  ngoam                = "enabled"
  nv_overlay           = "enabled"
  ospf                 = "enabled"
  ospfv3               = "enabled"
  pim                  = "enabled"
  ptp                  = "enabled"
  pvlan                = "enabled"
  sflow                = "enabled"
  scp_server           = "enabled"
  security_group       = "enabled"
  service_acceleration = "enabled"
  sftp_server          = "enabled"
  ssh                  = "enabled"
  tacacs               = "enabled"
  telemetry            = "enabled"
  telnet               = "enabled"
  udld                 = "enabled"
  vn_segment           = "enabled"
  vpc                  = "enabled"
  feature_sets = {
    "fex" = {
      admin_state = "enabled"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `analytics` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `bash_shell` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `bfd` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `bgp` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.
- `dhcp` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `evpn` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `feature_sets` (Attributes Map) List of feature-sets (e.g. `fex`, `mpls`, `virtualization`).
  - Map key: `name` - Feature-set name.
  - Key choices: `fex`, `mpls`, `virtualization` (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--feature_sets))
- `grpc` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `hmm` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `hsrp` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `interface_vlan` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The distinguished name of the object.

<a id="nestedatt--feature_sets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `feature_sets`

Optional:

- `admin_state` (String) Administrative state.
  - Choices: `none`, `enabled`, `disabled`, `installed`, `uninstalled`

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to       = nxos_feature.example
  identity = {}
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema


#### Optional

- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = nxos_feature.example
  id = ""
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import nxos_feature.example ""
```
